attractions.name: Accomplice Beer Company (Accomplice Beer Company)
attractions.description: Accomplice Beer Company was a unique brewery and restaurant located in the historic Cheyenne Depot building, combining historical surroundings with modern craft brewing technology. It featured a self-service tap wall concept where guests could pour their own beer and enjoyed a reputation for its distinct craft beers and elevated pub fare. (Kupibilet, Yahoo News)

attractions.days_open: Closed permanently as of January 31, 2026 (Accomplice Beer Company)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Big Boy Steam Engine 4004
attractions.description: Big Boy 4004 is one of only eight surviving 4-8-8-4 "Big Boy" steam locomotives, which were the largest ever built. It was manufactured by the American Locomotive Company in 1941 to haul heavy freight over the steep grades between Cheyenne and Ogden. Today, it stands as a massive 1.2-million-pound static display in Holliday Park, offering visitors a close-up look at a legendary piece of American rail history. (Visit Cheyenne, Little America)
attractions.hours: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM daily (Cheyenne Rec)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Cheyenne, Yelp)
highlights: "World's largest steam locomotive class (4-8-8-4) ([Visit Cheyenne)", 'One of only eight surviving examples in the world (WyoHistory.org)', 'Massive size: 132 feet long and weighing 1.2 million pounds (YouTube/Walk Around)', 'Historic role in transcontinental rail travel over Sherman Hill (YouTube/Walk Around)', 'Accessible for up-close viewing and photography in Holliday Park (Visit Cheyenne)']
attractions.tips: Visit during daylight hours for the best photographs of this massive locomotive. The engine is located in the southeast corner of Holliday Park and is a very short walk from the nearby parking lot. (Yelp, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Cheyenne Depot Museum (Depot Museum), Wyoming State Museum (Tripadvisor), Historic Governors' Mansion (Tripadvisor), Wyoming State Capitol (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Camp Carlin Military Site (Cheyenne Depot)
attractions.description: Camp Carlin, also known as Cheyenne Depot, was established in 1867 as a vital military supply hub for the northern High Plains during the Indian Wars. It served as the second-largest quartermaster depot in the United States, supplying fourteen Army posts and various Indian agencies with food, clothing, and equipment. Today, the site is commemorated by a historical marker and its legacy is preserved at the Warren ICBM & Heritage Museum. (WyoHistory, HMdb)
attractions.address: 4038 Happy Jack Road (marker), 401 Champagne Dr., Bldg. 31 (Museum)
attractions.hours: Museum: Monday-Friday 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Marker: Accessible 24/7 (Warren ICBM & Heritage Museum, HMdb)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Museum); Daily (Outdoor Marker) (Warren ICBM & Heritage Museum, HMdb)
attractions.admission: Free (Warren ICBM & Heritage Museum, HMdb)
highlights: 'Historical marker commemorating the second-largest military supply depot in the U.S. ([WyoHistory)', 'Preserved history and artifacts at the Warren ICBM & Heritage Museum (Warren ICBM & Heritage Museum)', 'Supplied 12–14 different Army posts including Forts Laramie, Phil Kearny, and McKinney (HMdb)', 'Vital logistics hub during the construction of the Union Pacific Railroad (Geology of Wyoming)', 'Historical site of the "Camp Carlin Supply Run" during annual Fort D.A. Russell Days (F.E. Warren AFB)', 'Home to 1,000 mules and 100 freight wagons at its peak (WyoHistory)']
attractions.tips: Visitors to the Warren ICBM & Heritage Museum must coordinate base access at least 72 hours in advance (90 days for foreign nationals) and provide a REAL ID. The outdoor historical marker on Happy Jack Road is easily accessible without base entry. (Warren ICBM & Heritage Museum, HMdb)
attractions.nearby: Fort D.A. Russell (F.E. Warren AFB), Curt Gowdy State Park, Terry Bison Ranch, Cheyenne Depot Museum (Tripadvisor, Geology of Wyoming)

attractions.name: Cheyenne Botanic Gardens (botanic.org)
attractions.description: The Cheyenne Botanic Gardens is a lush oasis in Wyoming’s capital, featuring the three-story Shane Smith Grand Conservatory, the LEED Platinum-certified Paul Smith Children’s Village, and nine acres of award-winning landscapes. As the only botanic garden in Wyoming, it exists to cultivate community by providing treasured gardens, educational opportunities, and enrichment in a challenging high-plains climate (botanic.org, Tripadvisor).

highlights: 'Shane Smith Grand Conservatory ([botanic.org)', 'Paul Smith Children’s Village (botanic.org)', 'Peace Garden (Travel Wyoming)', 'Engine 1242 (Oldest locomotive engine in Wyoming) (Tripadvisor)', 'High Plains Arboretum (botanic.org)', 'Solar-powered Tropical Greenhouse (Tripadvisor)']
attractions.tips: Bring water and sunscreen as the Children's Village and grounds are primarily outdoors; note that professional photography sessions require a reservation and fee. attractions.name: Cheyenne Botanic Gardens botanic.org

It offers a diverse collection of tropical plants, year-round activities, and the interactive Paul Smith Children’s Village, which focuses on sustainability and environmental education. It was renowned for its high-quality steaks and Western-themed ambiance, serving as a social and political hub for the state.

attractions.description: The Cheyenne Depot Museum is housed in the historic 1886 Union Pacific Railroad Depot, a National Historic Landmark. It offers a journey through the history of Cheyenne and the construction of the Transcontinental Railroad, featuring extensive exhibits and a renowned model railroad. The building stands as a testament to the city's railroad roots and serves as a major cultural center for downtown events.

Brunk', 'On-site Visit Cheyenne information kiosk for local travel assistance', 'Annual venue for major community events like Depot Days and the Celtic Festival']

attractions.name: Curt Gowdy State Park
attractions.description: Curt Gowdy State Park, nestled in the scenic foothills of the Laramie Mountains, offers a diverse landscape of rolling hills, low-lying meadows, and massive granite formations. The park is renowned for its three reservoirs—Granite, Crystal, and North Crow—providing exceptional opportunities for fishing, boating, and water sports. Visitors can also explore over 35 miles of world-class trails designated as an 'Epic' by the International Mountain Bicycling Association. Wyoming State Parks
attractions.hours: Park: 24 hours daily; Visitor Center: Monday-Sunday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Granite Fee Booth: Open daily (hours vary; Fri-Sat until 7:30 PM). Wyoming State Parks

[Visit Cheyenne', 'Historic Hynds Lodge and a 150-seat outdoor amphitheater listed on the National Register.', 'On-site archery range and free public horse corrals for visitors with horses.', 'Diverse wildlife viewing opportunities including mule deer and various bird species.']

attractions.name: First United Methodist Church of Cheyenne

attractions.name: Historic Cheyenne Air Terminal (1960 Terminal)
attractions.description: The Cheyenne Air Terminal is a historic mid-century modern facility renowned for its unique hyperbolic paraboloid (HyPar) roof design. Originally opened in 1960 to replace a 1929 structure, it served as the city's primary aviation hub for nearly 60 years and is currently being revitalized into a multi-use center for arts, culture, and aviation heritage. It is a significant cultural landmark that has hosted numerous presidents and dignitaries throughout its history.
attractions.hours: Typically 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM during open house events and scheduled festivals; otherwise closed for daily interior public access during ongoing renovations.
attractions.days_open: Open for special events and scheduled tours; exterior viewable daily.
attractions.admission: Free for general exterior viewing; specific events such as the Wyoming Aviation Festival may charge admission (e.g., $10 for adults, children free).

attractions.tips: Check the Arts Cheyenne or Wyoming Aviation Heritage League websites for special events like the Wyoming Aviation Festival to gain interior access. Be sure to visit the adjacent Airport Fountain Park to see the restored mid-century fountain. The exterior is best viewed from East 8th Avenue for the best perspective of the unique roofline.

attractions.name: Historic Lakeview Cemetery
attractions.description: Established in 1871 as City Cemetery, Lakeview Cemetery is the oldest city-owned cemetery in Cheyenne and serves as a repository of local and state history. It is the final resting place for numerous notable figures, including nearly every Wyoming governor, pioneers, and outlaws, reflecting the growth of the American West (City of Cheyenne, Visit Cheyenne).
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset (Grounds open 7 days a week); Office: Monday - Friday, 8:00 a.m. - 10:00 a.m. (City of Cheyenne).
attractions.admission: Free; Lakeview Cemetery Walk living history tours are $10 for adults and free for children 12 and under (Wyoming Public Media).
highlights: 'Final resting place of notable figures like Nellie Tayloe Ross (the first female governor in the U.S.) and Francis E. Warren ([Visit Cheyenne).', 'Home to the only Confederate soldier grave in Cheyenne, John C. Hunton (YouTube).', 'Features oversized and elaborate historical tombs and headstones dating back to the late 1800s (Bob Ruwart Motors Blog).', 'Site of the annual "Lakeview Cemetery Walk," a living history program where costumed volunteers portray historical figures (Wyoming Public Media).', "Awarded multiple historical preservation certificates for its role in promoting Wyoming's heritage (City of Cheyenne).", 'Contains graves of early pioneers, outlaws, and even a Native American woman and child (Tripadvisor).']
attractions.tips: Pick up a self-guided walking tour brochure at the main office or the cemetery gate to locate the most significant historical gravesites (Visit Cheyenne, Bob Ruwart Motors Blog). The cemetery is easy to navigate by both car and foot, with many pulling over to explore interesting epitaphs (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Cheyenne Regional Medical Center, Wyoming State Museum, Holliday Park, and the Wyoming Historic Governors' Mansion (Mapcarta, Evendo).

attractions.name: Holliday Park Visit Cheyenne
attractions.description: Holliday Park is a scenic community park in Cheyenne, Wyoming, known for its mix of historical landmarks and recreational facilities. It features the picturesque Lake Minnehaha and the massive Big Boy Steam Engine Old Number 4004, the world's largest steam locomotive. The park offers diverse amenities including tennis courts, playgrounds, and picnic shelters, making it a popular spot for families and railroad enthusiasts alike. Visit Cheyenne, Tripadvisor
attractions.address: 17th Street at Morrie Avenue, Cheyenne, WY 82001 Visit Cheyenne
attractions.hours: Daily 5:00 AM – 12:00 AM Cheyenne Rec
attractions.admission: Free Airial Travel
highlights: 'Big Boy Steam Engine (Old Number 4004) [Visit Cheyenne', 'Lake Minnehaha Denver Public Library', 'Kids Castle Playground City of Cheyenne', 'Cheyenne Artists Guild (Van Tassell Carriage Barn) Wyoming Public Media', 'Picnic Gazebos and Shelters Cheyenne Rec', 'Tennis and Basketball Courts Cheyenne Rec']
attractions.tips: Visit during daylight hours to see the Big Boy steam engine clearly, as it is not illuminated at night. The park can be busy on weekends, so arrive early to secure picnic spots or use of the shelters. Tripadvisor, Airial Travel
attractions.nearby: Cheyenne Depot Museum, Wyoming State Capitol, Cheyenne Botanic Gardens, Wyoming State Museum Visit Cheyenne

attractions.name: Jo-Ed Produce Inc. (also known as Jo Ed Produce)
attractions.description: Jo-Ed Produce Inc. is a long-standing food service contractor and wholesale produce distributor located in Cheyenne, Wyoming. Founded in 1940, the company specializes in providing a general line of groceries and fresh produce to the regional community, including schools, hospitals, and local businesses. It operates as a family-owned business and has been a recipient of federal contracts for food service distribution.

attractions.name: Laramie County Library System (Cheyenne Library)
attractions.description: The Laramie County Library System, founded in 1886, is the oldest continually operating county library system in the United States Wikipedia. Its award-winning main branch in Cheyenne is a 100,000-square-foot facility that serves as a vibrant community hub with three floors of resources and interactive spaces Laramie County Library System.

attractions.admission: Free Laramie County Library System

attractions.tips: Don't miss the rooftop weather station for panoramic views of the city. Families should prioritize the second floor, which is specifically designed with interactive exhibits for kids. The Library Café is a local favorite for fresh-baked goods and study snacks Visit Cheyenne.

attractions.name: Lions Park
attractions.description: Lions Park is a premier recreational destination in Cheyenne, spanning a large area that includes Sloan’s Lake and the renowned Cheyenne Botanic Gardens. Visitors can enjoy a variety of activities including boating, fishing, and exploring the multi-use trails that connect to the Greater Cheyenne Greenway. It is also designated as an Important Birding Area, supporting over 200 species of birds throughout the year (Visit Cheyenne).
attractions.address: 4603 Lions Park Dr, Cheyenne, WY 82001 (City of Cheyenne)
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Thursday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Friday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Saturday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Sunday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM (Cheyenne Rec)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit USA Parks)
highlights: "Sloan's Lake for boating, fishing, and beach activities ([Visit Cheyenne)", "Cheyenne Botanic Gardens and the Paul Smith Children's Village (Botanic.org)", 'Access to the Greater Cheyenne Greenway trail system (TrailLink)', 'Lions Park Amphitheater for outdoor performances (City of Cheyenne)', 'Cheyenne Aquatic Center and Municipal Pool (Visit Cheyenne)', 'Extensive playgrounds and rental picnic shelters (Cheyenne Rec)']
attractions.tips: The park is an Important Birding Area (IBA); bring binoculars to spot some of the 221 bird species recorded there. Rent a paddleboat or canoe at Sloan's Lake during the summer months for a scenic view of the park. Visit the Children's Village Tuesday through Sunday for family-friendly educational activities (Visit Cheyenne).
attractions.nearby: Cheyenne Frontier Days Old West Museum, Cheyenne Botanic Gardens, Paul Smith Children's Village, Airport Golf Club (Visit Cheyenne)

attractions.name: Luxury Diner
attractions.description: The Luxury Diner is a historic trolley-car-turned-diner that has been a staple of Cheyenne since 1926. This authentic piece of Americana is known for its "made-from-scratch" comfort food and a nostalgic atmosphere filled with railroad memorabilia. Food Network, The Wyoming Truth
attractions.hours: Monday – Thursday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Friday – Sunday: 6:00 AM – 3:00 PM The Wyoming Truth
attractions.admission: Free (Menu items range from approximately $6.00 to $16.00) AllMenus, The Wyoming Truth
highlights: 'Housed in a repurposed 1894 trolley car [Food Network', 'Famous for their homemade chicken-fried steak with sausage gravy Food Network', 'Signature Santa Fe Breakfast Burrito smothered in green chili The Wyoming Truth', 'Authentic railroad-themed decor and memorabilia Chill Travelers', 'Served by generations of locals in a classic diner setting The Wyoming Truth']
attractions.tips: Look for the vintage "Cheyenne Motel" neon sign to find the diner's entrance. It is a popular local spot, so be prepared for a potential wait during peak breakfast hours. Don't miss trying their signature green chili. Official Website, Food Network, The Wyoming Truth
attractions.nearby: Cheyenne Ice and Events Center, Wyoming State Museum, Cheyenne Depot Museum, Cheyenne Big Boots Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Nelson Museum of the West
attractions.description: The Nelson Museum of the West is a premier cultural institution in Cheyenne dedicated to preserving the diverse heritage of the Old West. Its extensive collection of over 14,000 artifacts includes Native American beadwork, 19th-century firearms, cowboy gear, and fine Western art, alongside a significant military collection housed in an extension across the street. (Official Site, Visit Cheyenne)
attractions.hours: May, Sept., & Oct.: Monday-Friday 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; June, July, & Aug.: Monday-Saturday 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Closed Sundays and holidays; Closed for season Nov. 1 - May 1 except for special events. (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Monday-Friday (May, Sept, Oct); Monday-Saturday (June-Aug); Closed to the public Nov-Apr except for special events. (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Adults: $5, Seniors: $4, Children (12 and under): Free, Groups (5+): $2 per person. Note: Admission is cash only. (Visit Cheyenne, Chamber of Commerce)
highlights: 'Extensive Native American collection featuring beadwork, basketry, and pottery ([Official Site)', 'Military Memorial Museum extension honoring service members from the 1800s through WWII (Official Site)', "19th-century Wyoming Cattle Baron's room and the studio of artist Burt Procter (Official Site)", 'Diverse taxidermy collection including lions, bears, and other big game animals (Visit Cheyenne)', 'Collection of over 14,000 artifacts including saddles, spurs, and firearms (Official Site)']
attractions.tips: The museum does not accept credit cards, so be sure to bring cash for admission. Visitors should plan to spend at least 90 minutes to 3 hours to fully explore the three floors of exhibits. Check out the Military Memorial Museum located directly across the street for a more comprehensive experience. (Yelp, Visit Cheyenne)
attractions.nearby: Cheyenne Depot Museum, Wyoming State Museum, Wyoming State Capitol, Cheyenne Botanic Gardens, Cheyenne Frontier Days Old West Museum. (Visit USA Parks, Reddit)

attractions.name: Peppermill Bar & Liquors (Stillwater Ave Area)
attractions.description: The Peppermill Bar & Liquors is a well-known local establishment in Cheyenne, featuring a full-service bar and a retail liquor store. It offers a relaxed atmosphere for locals and visitors to enjoy a wide selection of beverages and pub-style food. (Visit Cheyenne)

attractions.description: Poor Richard's is a long-standing fine dining establishment in Cheyenne, known for its elegant atmosphere and high-quality cuisine. Originally opened in 1977, it has become a local staple for those seeking a sophisticated yet family-friendly dining experience featuring premium steaks and seafood. Bob Ruwart Motors, Kid City Guide

attractions.name: Rainsford Inn Bed & Breakfast Tripadvisor
attractions.description: The Rainsford Inn is a historic bed and breakfast located in the heart of the Rainsford Historic District, once known as "Cattle Baron Row." The inn is housed in a beautifully preserved Victorian-style building that reflects the opulent history of Cheyenne's early cattle industry and architectural heritage Wikipedia, Tripadvisor.

attractions.admission: Room rates typically range from $125 to $200 per night, depending on the season and room type Tripadvisor

attractions.tips: Book in advance during Cheyenne Frontier Days, as local lodging fills up quickly. Take a self-guided walking tour of the surrounding historic district to see other "Cattle Baron" homes Travel on the Reg Visit Cheyenne

attractions.description: Sanford's Grub & Pub is a lively destination for southern comfort food and Cajun cuisine, featuring an eclectic decor inspired by the owner's love for the show 'Sanford and Son'. The restaurant offers a massive menu including handcrafted burgers, hand-cut steaks, and 55 beers on tap in a space filled with memorabilia and sports decor (Official Website, Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Southeast Wyoming Welcome Center
attractions.description: The Southeast Wyoming Welcome Center is a state-of-the-art facility that serves as a gateway to the Cowboy State. It features an interactive museum with a full-size mastodon skeleton and exhibits on Wyoming's natural resources, history, and culture. The center also provides travelers with maps, brochures, and helpful local advice from on-site specialists. (Travel Wyoming, Seek Discover Learn)
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Tripadvisor, Visit Cheyenne Guide)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Cheyenne, Trip.com)
highlights: ['Full-size Mastodon skeleton known as "Dee"', "Interactive museum displays on Wyoming's history and geography", 'Sustainable building design featuring solar power and wind turbines', 'Outdoor discovery gardens and fossilized Dawn Redwood tree', 'Comprehensive selection of state travel guides and maps', 'On-site pet exercise area and picnic facilities']
attractions.tips: Ask the front desk for a scavenger hunt list to engage children in the museum exhibits. Be prepared for high winds when exiting your vehicle, and keep an eye on the surrounding plains for sightings of local wildlife. (Visit Cheyenne, Yelp)

attractions.name: Terry Bison Ranch Resort
attractions.description: Terry Bison Ranch Resort is a ranch resort and Western adventure destination located on the Wyoming/Colorado state line. It offers a unique variety of activities including famous bison train tours, horseback trail rides, and a steakhouse, providing a true Western experience for families and groups. Visitors can enjoy up-close encounters with bison and explore the historic ranch that dates back to 1881 (Official Site, Visit USA Parks).
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu: 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Fri-Sun: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM (Ticketing & Gift Shop Hours) (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Open 7 days a week (Closed Christmas Day) (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Bison Train Tours: $20.00 Adults, $10.00 Children (4-12), Ages 3 and under Free; One Hour Trail Rides: $90.00; Pony Rides: $20.00; Self-Guided Walking Tour: Free; Fishing: $21.50 (up to 3 fish) (Official Site, City of Cheyenne PDF)
highlights: "America's only Bison Train Tour where visitors can feed the herd by hand ([Tripadvisor)", 'Guided horseback trail rides and pony rides available year-round (Official Site)', "On-site dining at Senator's Steakhouse and Brass Buffalo Saloon (City of Cheyenne PDF)", 'A variety of exotic animals to see, including camels, ostriches, and alpacas (Official Site)', 'Accommodations including historic cabins, RV sites, and tent camping (Official Site)', 'Seasonal activities such as ATV tours and fishing in a private stocked lake (City of Cheyenne PDF)']
attractions.tips: Most activities like train tours and trail rides are first-come, first-serve with no reservations needed; arrive at least 20 minutes before departure. Trail riders must be at least 8 years old and weigh under 250 lbs. Be sure to buy 'bison cakes' at the Trading Post to feed the bison on the train tour (Official Site, City of Cheyenne PDF).

attractions.description: The Albany is an established downtown business with a loyal customer following that has been serving Cheyenne for over 80 years. This historic restaurant and bar offers an old-world atmosphere with original 1942 Philippine mahogany booths and is known for its made-from-scratch American fare.

attractions.description: The BEAST Facility is a 28,000-square-foot multi-purpose recreational center operated by the City of Cheyenne’s Recreation Division. It serves as a hub for community sports, featuring regulation courts for basketball, volleyball, and pickleball, alongside a lounge and multipurpose rooms. The facility hosts both youth and adult programming, including seasonal leagues, fitness classes, and educational workshops (City of Cheyenne).

attractions.name: The Cheyenne Club (inspired by the historic Cheyenne Club)
attractions.description: The Cheyenne Club is a sophisticated fine-dining destination inspired by the legendary 19th-century social club for cattle barons. Located at The Farm at Brush Creek, it features a fusion of rustic elegance and modern Western aesthetic, highlighting ingredients sourced from an on-site greenhouse and the ranch's own Wagyu beef program. (Brush Creek Ranch, Venue Report)

It has historically hosted celebrities and business titans, and today it remains a vital piece of the city's culture and character. The Historic Plains Hotel, Haunted Rooms America

attractions.description: Union Pacific 844 is the last steam locomotive built for Union Pacific and the only steam locomotive never retired by a North American Class I railroad. Delivered in 1944, this high-speed passenger engine now serves as Union Pacific's "Living Legend" and ambassador of goodwill, traveling across the country for special excursions and public displays. attractions.name: Wyoming Brewers Festival
attractions.description: The Wyoming Brewers Festival is an annual fundraising event dedicated to the preservation of the Historic Union Pacific Depot in Downtown Cheyenne. Celebrating its 30th anniversary in 2026, the festival brings together over 40 craft breweries from Wyoming, Colorado, and beyond for two days of tasting, live music, and community spirit. All proceeds from the event directly support the ongoing restoration and maintenance of the iconic Depot landmark. Wyoming Brewers Festival Home, The Gist

highlights: ['Tastings from over 40 craft breweries across Wyoming, Colorado, and other states.', 'Directly supports the preservation and rehabilitation of the Historic Union Pacific Depot.', 'Features live music and food vendors throughout the event.', 'First Pour VIP session on Saturday offering exclusive early access and brewer interactions.', 'Commemorative 5oz beer mug and voting tokens included with general admission.', 'Interactive digital welcome kiosks and historic railroad exhibits at the Cheyenne Depot Museum nearby.']

attractions.name: Wyoming Hereford Ranch (Official Website)
attractions.description: Established in 1883, the Wyoming Hereford Ranch is the oldest continuous registered livestock operation in the United States. It is a historic working cattle ranch that offers scenic views of lush meadows and prairies, and it serves as a premier venue for weddings and community events. Visitors can explore the grounds through self-guided tours, visit the on-site library, or participate in world-class bird watching (Official Website, Visit Cheyenne).

attractions.region: East Cheyenne / Southeastern Laramie County (Zola, Cheyenne-High Plains Audubon Society)
attractions.hours: The Don Ornduff Memorial Library is open to the public seven days a week; general visitors are encouraged to visit during daylight hours and stop at the main office for information (Official Website, Visit Cheyenne).
attractions.admission: Free for visiting, self-guided tours, and bird watching (Visit Cheyenne, Cheyenne-High Plains Audubon Society). Venue rentals range from $2,000 to $5,000 (Official Website).
highlights: 'Oldest continuous registered livestock operation in the United States ([Official Website)', 'Don Ornduff Memorial Library featuring rare books on Western history and Hereford cattle (Official Website)', "Designated as an 'Important Bird Area' (IBA) by Audubon Wyoming with over 80 species observed (Official Website)", 'Historic architectural sites including the Sale Barn, Horse Barn, and Events Center (Official Website)', 'Self-guided walking tours available through maps provided at the main office (Visit Cheyenne)', 'High-quality Hereford beef raised on-site and available for direct purchase (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Visitors should first stop at the main office to pick up a self-guided tour map. It is essential to stay on established roads, avoid private residences, and refrain from crossing fences to respect the working ranch operations. Please note that dogs are not allowed on the property (Visit Cheyenne, Cheyenne-High Plains Audubon Society).

attractions.name: Wyoming State Capitol
attractions.description: The Wyoming State Capitol is a Renaissance Revival masterpiece and a National Historic Landmark, serving as the seat of Wyoming's executive and legislative branches. Built between 1886 and 1890, the building is renowned for its iconic gold-leaf dome and serves as a symbol of the state's heritage and commitment to equality. Following an extensive four-year restoration completed in 2019, the 'People’s House' offers visitors a chance to explore its grand chambers, historic vaults, and intricate architectural details.
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.; Saturday (May 23 - September 26): 9:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.
attractions.days_open: Monday-Friday (Year-round); Saturdays (Late May to Late September)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: 'Designated as one of only 20 state capitols in the nation as a National Historic Landmark ([Travel Wyoming)', 'Renaissance Revival architecture featuring a majestic copper dome covered in 24-karat gold leaf (Wikipedia)', "Interior murals by Allen Tupper True depicting scenes of Wyoming's history, including trappers and railroad builders (Wikipedia)", 'Historical walk-in safes on the Garden Level, once used to store valuable cargo (Trying To Unwind)', 'The Esther Hobart Morris Statue, honoring the first woman to hold judicial office in modern times, stands in front of the building (Tripadvisor)', 'Stunning interior rotunda with checkerboard marble floors and Corinthian-style columns (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Self-guided tours are available year-round with interpretive signage; photography for personal use is permitted (Travel Wyoming). Visitors can get their 'Capitol Collection Passport Book' stamped at the nearby Wyoming State Museum or the Historic Governor's Mansion (Wyoming A&I). Public parking is available on the streets surrounding the Capitol and in a state parking lot at 25th Street and Pioneer Avenue (Wyoming Capitol Square).

attractions.name: Ames Monument State Historic Site (Wyoming State Parks)
attractions.description: The Ames Monument is a 60-foot-tall granite pyramid built in 1882 to honor Oakes and Oliver Ames, key financiers of the first transcontinental railroad. It was designed by renowned architect Henry Hobson Richardson and stands at the highest point of the railroad's original route through the Sherman Hill area (Wyoming State Parks).

attractions.hours: Daily, sunrise to sunset (Wyoming State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (Wyoming State Parks), (Visit Laramie)

attractions.tips: The site is located down a dirt road, so use caution during inclement weather. It is often very windy at the summit, so dress accordingly and bring a jacket. Visiting during sunrise or sunset offers excellent lighting for photography of the granite structure (Tripadvisor), (Evendo).

attractions.name: Lander Brewing Company
attractions.description: Lander Brewing Company is a historic microbrewery re-established in 2011, continuing a tradition that began in the late 1800s. Located in the heart of downtown Lander as part of the Coalter Block, it produces award-winning craft beers using exceptional water sourced from the Wind River Mountains. Official Website, Wyoming Beer Trail

attractions.name: Laramie Peak Museum
attractions.description: The Laramie Peak Museum is a local history museum that preserves the rich heritage of Platte County and the surrounding region. Its exhibits feature an extensive collection of artifacts from early homesteaders, the Oregon Trail, and the cattle baron era, providing a window into the lives of the pioneers who settled in southeastern Wyoming.

It winds along the Laramie River, offering a relaxed environment for walking, cycling, and wildlife viewing right within the city limits. (City of Laramie, Travel Wyoming)

The area near Cheyenne is highlighted by the Pole Mountain unit, featuring the unique Sherman Granite rock formations of Vedauwoo and the extensive multi-use trail networks of the Happy Jack Recreation Area. Visit Cheyenne, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Sinks Canyon State Park
attractions.description: Sinks Canyon State Park is a unique geologic wonder located in the Wind River Range, famous for the Middle Fork of the Popo Agie River which vanishes into a limestone cavern and reappears a quarter-mile downstream. The park offers diverse recreational opportunities including hiking, world-class rock climbing, and camping amidst scenic canyon walls and diverse wildlife habitat. (Wyoming State Parks, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Park Facilities: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM daily; Visitor Center: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM daily (May 22 – September 7) (Wyoming State Parks)
attractions.days_open: Open year-round; Visitor Center open seasonally from late May to early September (Wyoming State Parks)
attractions.admission: Resident Daily Use: $7.00 per vehicle; Non-Resident Daily Use: $12.00 per vehicle; Resident Camping: $10.00; Non-Resident Camping: $18.00; Cave Tours: $20.00 per person (Wyoming State Parks, Wind River Visitors Council)
highlights: ['The Sinks: A limestone cavern where the Popo Agie River disappears underground.', 'The Rise: A peaceful pool where the river resurfaces, home to massive trout.', 'Trout Feeding: A popular activity where visitors can feed large brown and rainbow trout from an observation deck.', 'Popo Agie Falls: A scenic 3.4-mile roundtrip hike leading to beautiful waterfalls.', 'Boulder Choke Cave: Guided tours of a multi-level cave system (seasonal).', 'International Dark Sky Park: Recognized for its exceptional stargazing opportunities.']
attractions.tips: Bring quarters to purchase fish food for the trout at the Rise; the water takes about two hours to travel from the Sinks to the Rise despite the short distance. Proper food storage is mandatory in campgrounds to prevent bear encounters. (Wind River Visitors Council, Geology of Wyoming)

It is world-renowned for its unique wind-sculpted hoodoos and technical rock climbing opportunities, offering a mix of developed and walk-in campsites at an elevation of 8,300 feet (US Forest Service, Recreation.gov)

Now a museum and state park, the site offers a glimpse into early frontier justice and includes the original prison building, the warden's house, and a historic broom factory. (Official Site, Wikipedia)
